steps.title: Find the line from the table
steps.content: This asks for $b$ in a linear model, so use two table points directly. From $(1,46)$ to $(2,43)$, the slope is $m=\frac{43-46}{2-1}=-3.$ Substituting a point into $y=mx+b$ gives $b=49$.

steps.title: Confirm the requested value
steps.content: The model is $g(x)=-3x+49$. The question asks for the constant $b$, which is the y-intercept of the line, so $b=49$. A quick check: $g(1)=-3+49=46$ and $g(3)=-9+49=40$, matching the table. The answer is 49.
